utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the final format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present with physical and mental health insurance and costs. Includes providers and costs; enhancements detailed with cost. Fully specified per requirement. 2) property_coverage: Present as home/contents insurance with costs, provider, details and add-ons with costs. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: Present with provider, costs, details; enhancements with costs and discounts. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: Present with services, coverage details, and costs. Fully specified. 5) total_budget: Present with current total, enhancement addition, revised total, and notes. Fully specified. Therefore all 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I scanned the final coverage plan and compared each named item against the ground-truth catalog. Many entries in the plan use providers or package names that do not appear in the ground-truth lists (e.g., Bradesco Executive International, ExecutiveCare Wellness, PremiumSecure Luxury, ExecutiveGlobal Platinum, Concierge Medical Services, Enhanced Stress Management Package, Elite Executive Protection Package, Executive Insurance Concierge). Only items that exactly match names in the ground-truth can be mapped to ratings. None of the plan’s items match the ground-truth option names for primary health (e.g., EliteCare Premium Plan, PrimeCare Premium Plan), property (e.g., EliteHome Premium Coverage, PrimeHome Premium Coverage), travel (e.g., PrimeTravel Premium Plan, EliteTravel Premium Plan), or the enhancement/general services lists. Therefore, there are no mappable items, and the RATINGS section must be empty.
